mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and relieves financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when policy is easing rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward surprise in retail sales indicates stronger near-term consumption and revenue momentum, which lifts earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses after the RSAFS crossing condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and consumption demand, so equity prices should fall over the following month as earnings expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of instances after the unemployment rate rises by 0.2 pp or more over 2 months, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is stable, firms are expanding real output without a parallel inflation shock, which supports margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and flat CPIAUCSL does not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the sign equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and PAYEMS marks broad demand weakening and labor deterioration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with falling GDPC1 and PAYEMS do not coincide with below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate compresses equity valuations through discount-rate pressure, but the effect is strongest only when inflation is also elevated; the joint tightening-inflation regime predicts negative short-horizon returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate/high-inflation trigger does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the effect is absent when CPIAUCSL inflation is below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates payroll contraction, reduced household income, and weaker future spending, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly payroll declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most instances, or if the signal loses sign consistency across successive downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness signals improving housing demand and broader cyclical momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month average after being below it for at least 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds do not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is inverted during periods when HOUST is already above trend.
